summary: Approximately half of Intermediate Density Lipoproteins (IDLs) get metabolized via conversion to Low-Density Lipoproteins (LDL), whereas about an equivalent amount gets cleared through direct uptake by hepatocytes for processing or degradation within these cells themselves. The fate distribution aligning closely matches what was previously proposed based primarily around empirical observations supported now further substantiated across multiple studies indicating similar percentages being directed towards either outcome post-formation from VLDL remnants during lipid metabolism processes occurring predominantly after meals when triglyceride-rich chylomicrons have been processed initially leading them eventually transforming partially into more dense forms including both types mentioned above before their ultimate clearance mechanisms kick-in depending largely off individual metabolic states among other factors influencing overall cholesterol homeostasis dynamics observed clinically over time periods ranging typically days-to-weeks given typical physiological
